reviews.text:
I'll begin my review by stating the following:
Dan Estrada helped me achieve way more than I could have achieved on my own.
With Estrada's help, the uphill battle became much more manageable.

He kept me sane, and kept me on track on how to present my case.
He also gave me realistic answers - he didn't mince with words and gave me the real facts of what we were facing, what we would have to prepare, best and worst case scenarios.
And a definite plus on his part, he also kept me from going way off the OCD charts by keeping me focused on what to prepare and what to leave on the backburner.

I spoke with several recommended lawyers from friends, associates and coworkers, and yet none of them were willing to take this case due to the difficulty.
Estrada decided to take it on, and considered it a challenge.

I would highly recommend Dan Estrada's services to any friends and family who may find themselves in need of legal help.  I'm certainly glad I did.
He  has stayed in contact with his clients.
Bailey is a consummate professional, I’d highly recommend him!

I recently contacted the law office of J Bailey McShane in a possible issue I thought was going to turn into a situation I needed his help on. He was able to guide me through what to expect and what was impressive to me was he actually checked in on me several times to make sure I was okay. Luckily the issue didnt turn into a situation and in my experience of working with other law firms, this law firm actually checked on me lastnight after 9pm and again this morning to make sure I was okay when others did not do that after 5pm nor did they check in on me next day. That said I saved this attorneys number in my phone in case I ever need them in the future. Thank you for being there when nobody else was available and for the advice you gave me. That very valuable to me to have an attorney that proactively checks on people that reach out for help.

reviews.ownerResponse: We respect all of our clients and work very hard to get them disability benefits. We are happy to answer any questions from our clients. Unfortunately, some people have a habit of being very rude or abusive to staff, raising their voice, using an accusatory or preachy tone, or being belligerent. We can't tolerate such behavior. Instead of calling us in an irate mood, please calm down to speak to staff civilly or ask to speak to one of our attorneys. It happens often that clients speak to random employees of their local SSA office and get misinformation and blame aimed in their attorney's direction. The information received from SSA in these circumstances is almost always wrong or incomplete. Your attorney is almost never responsible for delaying the processing of your claim. Right now there is a backlog in the SSA's processing time due to a massive staffing shortage that occurred during Covid. This has caused SSA's processing of cases in Texas to be delayed significantly. We explain this delay to everyone we sign. It is very frustrating for the claimants and us as well, but it is not something we can control. So, please be polite to staff when you call. They are human beings too and we cannot pay them enough money to tolerate verbal abuse.

I want to share my sincere gratitude to Mike Howard and his team for helping me through one of the most stressful and unfair situations of my life. Before finding Mike, I had worked with another lawyer who didn’t communicate well and made me feel hopeless. But when I hired Mike, everything changed. Throughout the process, Mike and his staff were incredibly kind, responsive, and compassionate. Every time I called or emailed, they were patient and reassuring. Mike kept me informed the entire time, and his calm confidence gave me peace when I was losing sleep from worry.

When I finally received confirmation that my case was officially rejected, I was overwhelmed with gratitude. I knew it was only possible because of Mike’s dedication, intelligence, and persistence. He truly cared about my situation and fought for me as if it were his own life on the line.

It’s rare to find a lawyer who is both exceptionally skilled and genuinely caring, but Mike Howard is exactly that. He’s honest, passionate, and deeply committed to helping people through their darkest moments. If anyone reading this is facing a criminal accusation — especially if you feel misunderstood or unfairly accused — I can tell you from experience: you can trust Mike Howard completely.

reviews.text: I am on probation trying to piece my life back together, from what was left of it after going through the eye of the court system. I missed 4 consecutive setting from my therapist counselor, not because I didn’t want to attend classes but because the therapist counselor and his assistant was giving me conflicting info. The assistant would tell me to attend class but when i attend class and I met with the therapist counselor he said I wasn’t supposed to be there that my initial intake was not complete.They Discharged me unsuccessfully from my class. My PO immediately file to get my Probation revoked. I made contact to Mike Howard and Associates.  Don’t be scared of the fee. Mike office was very reasonable, they work with me to get my account paid off in full. Mike’s office knew exactly what I was going through and what was to come. They kept me informed. Mike acted immediately, he went and set things straight with the probation office in court. Probation court mentioned to me that my counselor therapist wrote on my file saying I was a high risk and danger to the community, just because I didn’t show up to four class sessions. Mike got the court to issue a Probation Violation summons in court to discuss the violation instead of a warrant for my arrest and revocation of probation. During the PV summons Mike talked to the probation court and they granted me to continue on with my probation with a warning. Contact Mike’s office if you’re ever in trouble. Mike and his Office are competent and they know how to  handle criminal cases. I wished I have gone with Mike’s office while I was going through my initial hearing and pretrial hearing of my court case. I know I didn’t commit this crime. When your in court it doesn’t matter if you did it or not. He said she said you did it, it must be true; that is the song the prosecutors want to hear. And that’s the song they will play. Passing a Polygraph don’t matter either. Plea deal will be place in your lap. Update:
Its been 3 weeks since I terminated silberman law, and they've failed to file Notice of Withdrawal of Counsel with the probate court. Because this basic step was never completed, the court still lists them as my attorney of record, which has blocked my new counsel from appearing and delayed the probate even further.

My new attorney has now had to personally contact Silberman Law Firm to request that they file the withdrawal, which should have been handled immediately when representation ended. This has created additional work for my new attorney. Their ongoing administrative negligence continues to create unnecessary hurdles and wastes valuable time in a case that has already been significantly delayed.

I hired Silberman Law Firm for a simple, uncontested probate and paid over $7,000. After 18 months, four attorneys, and a mountain of repeated mistakes, the case stalled completely with no Letters of Administration ever obtained. I ultimately filed a formal complaint with the State Bar of Texas after discovering that my client file included a  probate document belonging to another client. When I brought this to supervising attorney Chris Heald, he dismissed it outright, denied it's existence, and refused to take responsibility. Once he refused to thoroughly review the case file himself, I submitted this document to the bar for formal review.

Mr. Heald’s attitude throughout my case was consistently dismissive and unprofessional. As supervising attorney, he is responsible for overseeing his associates but refused to acknowledge even basic filing errors. In February, Mr. Heald misled me to believe that the case was ready for hearing and requested that I replenish the retainer so that we could proceed with the hearing. Two attorneys, several months, multiple redrafts and corrections later, P. longo requested another retainer.

After P. Longo’s departure, I spoke with Mr. Heald to have my case reassigned to yet another attorney. When I raised concerns about the lack of progress and the firm’s billing practices, he brushed me off, told me he couldn’t do anything about their attorney turnover and insisted the case was “ready for hearing.” At this time, I had already spoken directly with the clerk, who told me the citations were paused as the filings were again, defective. Mr. Heald stated that if the work was billed, it was done. Just know that if you decide to hire this firm, the financial burden associated with their inability to keep their attorneys is apparently the client's responsibility.

The attorneys assigned to my case — Michael Sheetz, M. Rogers, Patricia Longo, and G. Cox — each had to relearn and redo the same work. Every transition meant new file reviews, new drafts, new filings, and MORE CHARGES. Meanwhile, communication was inconsistent, and I had to continually chase down basic information. I even had to call the county clerk myself on multiple occasions, as P. Longo in particular was difficult to reach, and when she finally responded, she was not transparent or forthcoming about the errors she had made or why the case was stalled. Even after I signed corrected documents (and was billed for them), the versions filed with the court did not reflect those corrections.

These unresolved mistakes ultimately halted the probate. By the time the final attorney, G. Cox, took over, he repeatedly stated that he’d never seen such a client file and still could not explain the status of my case after two weeks of (requested) check-ins.

When I was searching for a new attorney to handle my probate matter, multiple independent probate attorneys reviewed the filings and advised that the entire matter would need to be restarted from the beginning because the work was defective. Due to these delays, the estate has now incurred over $1,700 in tax penalties. I asked for a refund and was denied.

All in all, this was an expensive and terrible experience during a time that is already extremely difficult for myself and my family after losing my father, and no progress was made in probating his estate.

We retained this law firm with a $700 payment, covering two hours of work, for a straightforward real estate transaction—purchasing a home for a family member. Our direct interactions with the attorney consisted of two brief phone calls totaling about 20 minutes. Additionally, we received a four-page document outlining potential options, but it was largely a simple copy-and-paste job, offering minimal depth. In fact, approximately 80% of the information provided was basic and readily available on the internet.

During our discussion of these options, the attorney left us with unanswered questions and assured us that she would "research and follow up"—but that never happened. After receiving no response to my follow-up email, I escalated the issue and was referred to the lead attorney. He called me once, but when I attempted to return his call, I received no response. When I finally did get in touch, he claimed the case was closed—despite the fact that our questions remained unresolved.
